title: Configure MDX/JSON content ingestion from /content directory
summary: Install and configure next-mdx-remote or @next/mdx to compile MDX files under /content at build time. Add a utility module (e.g. lib/content.ts) that reads and parses all MDX and JSON section files from /content, returning typed Section objects. Ensure JSON files are imported with correct TypeScript typings.
acceptance_criteria: next-mdx-remote or @next/mdx is installed and configured in next.config.js
lib/content.ts exports a getAllSections() function returning typed Section[]
MDX files in /content are compiled to React components at build time
JSON files in /content are parsed and returned as typed objects

related_requirements.text: All slide content sourced from local JSON/MDX at build time with zero server-side runtime.
The build pipeline reads all slide data from /content at build time and bakes it into the static bundle.
A client-side state machine manages the current slide index, updates the URL hash, and responds to keyboard arrow key events.
Demo slide video elements auto-load from /public; the presenter can play, pause, and resume without leaving the slide.
Vercel project is configured with build command `next build && next export` and output directory `out`; the generated Vercel URL must be publicly accessible.
The Next.js project must be bootstrapped and maintained in TypeScript with strict mode enabled.

Sections must include: personal intro, AI overview (neural net → AI building → unattended agentic AI), prod-ready agent architecture, legacy vs reimagined workflow demo, maintenance/cost/audit considerations, Stripe Minions and Ramp examples, and agentic DevOps differentiator with uptime metric.
Typing or sharing a hash URL (e.g. #qa) must navigate the client-side state machine to the target slide without reloading the page.
A pre-build or CI check measures total size of /public and aborts the build with a clear error if the limit is exceeded.
Each slide fills the full viewport; no vertical or horizontal scrollbars appear during a standard presentation session.
